lede: From LVMH declaring generative engine optimization a strategic priority at VivaTech to AI virtual try-on delivering 10x luxury conversion lifts — this week's signal is clear: the battle for luxury discovery has moved from Google to ChatGPT.

stories.headline: LVMH Crowns Bluefish 'Most Promising' at VivaTech as Louis Vuitton Goes Live Inside ChatGPT — GEO Is Now a Group Priority
stories.summary: At VivaTech's 10th edition, LVMH handed its Most Promising Innovation Award to Bluefish, a GEO startup that measures and optimises brand representation across ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity. LVMH's chief omnichannel officer Gonzague de Pirey stated 'a significant number of customers are now going to LLM first,' framing AI search as an upstream discovery crisis. Simultaneously, Louis Vuitton unveiled a ChatGPT-integrated app for natural-language product discovery, Dior launched a formal GEO workstream with Bluefish, and Sephora confirmed a full ChatGPT integration for recommendation-to-checkout commerce. The group drew a sharp line: generative AI is confined to internal ops and discovery — never customer-facing creative or advertising.

stories.headline: DressX Data: AI Virtual Try-On Lifts Luxury Conversion Up to 10x — The Performance Case Is Now Impossible to Ignore
stories.summary: DressX's latest intelligence report, surfaced this week by Business of Fashion, quantifies what luxury e-commerce teams have been debating: shoppers who engage with AI virtual try-on are 50% more likely to purchase overall, while conversion rates among luxury consumers jump up to 10 times higher compared to those who do not interact with the feature. The data lands as LVMH, Dior, and Victoria Beckham are all in active deployment conversations with the platform. With digital try-on moving from novelty to performance lever, luxury CMOs now face a direct ROI argument — one that is harder to dismiss than any previous tech pitch.

stories.headline: Cannes Lions 2026 Debuts the Creative Brand Lion — AB InBev Wins the Inaugural Grand Prix for Systematising Creativity at Scale
stories.summary: The 73rd Cannes Lions opened Day 1 by awarding its brand-new Creative Brand Lion Grand Prix to AB InBev's 'Creativity at Scale,' recognising not a campaign but the internal systems, cultures, and capabilities that make award-level creative work structurally repeatable across a global portfolio. The category signals a formal industry shift: awards are now being given for creative infrastructure, not just outputs — a frame directly applicable to luxury conglomerates managing 70+ maisons. Day 1 also flagged 'AI-enabled utility' as a recurring jury theme, while The Ordinary's Health & Wellness Grand Prix demonstrated the power of science-backed creative tearing down beauty industry obfuscation.

stories.headline: Cannes Lions Day 3: Media, Direct, PR and Social Creator Lions Awarded — Vaseline Leads Asia as Platform-Native Creativity Dominates
stories.summary: Day 3 of Cannes Lions 2026 delivered Grand Prix across the Media, Direct, PR, and Social & Creator Lions — the categories most directly relevant to how luxury brands allocate paid and earned media investment. Juries rewarded platform-native work built for cultural specificity and measurable participation, not broadcast interruption. The Social & Creator category, now a major strategic lens for luxury houses navigating creator-led brand storytelling, produced standout winners that blended earned and owned media seamlessly. For luxury media teams, the signal is consistent across all three days: measurable cultural impact and systemic creative infrastructure are the new benchmarks.

stories.headline: VivaTech 2026: Sephora Embeds Full Purchase Journey in ChatGPT; Dior Builds Agentic In-Store AI Advisor — Conversational Commerce Arrives for Luxury
stories.summary: Detailed demonstrations at VivaTech 2026 revealed the operational depth of LVMH's conversational AI push. Sephora's ChatGPT integration — live in the US and Canada — allows users to receive recommendations, send photos, and complete purchases without leaving the conversation window. Dior is separately developing an AI agent that not only recommends product but prepares a personalised in-store visit briefing. Céline's sales advisors now use an AI assistant built on LVMH's internal Maya model to handle customer requests in real time, including photo-based product identification. LVMH's stated objective remains steering LLM-discovered clients back into physical stores — conversational AI as a funnel to the boutique, not a replacement for it.
